hub_subtitle: Business English from the ground up -- your work, your team, your routine

lesson.menu_desc: Checkpoint of Lessons 9-15: run a full workday in English, start to finish -- 29 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 16 -- Review: Lessons 9-15 -- A Simulated Workday in English
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Danilo Febba | Aula 16 -- Review: Lessons 9-15

listenings.text: Hi, I am Rachel. Let me tell you about a normal workday. My shift starts at nine o'clock. First, I check the agenda for the day. I can see all my meetings on the calendar, and my top priority is the morning report. At ten, I am usually on a phone call with the team. I like talking to my colleagues, and I enjoy solving small problems. At noon, I take a short break and catch up with a friend. In the afternoon, I sort out any new tasks. Finally, at five, I wrap up my work and write a short summary. It is a busy day, but I am happy.

Hello, this is Danilo. Let me give a quick overview of my workday. I am a data engineering lead, so I manage a small team. My shift starts early. First, I open my laptop and check the agenda. My top priority is always the data report. In the morning, I am often in meetings. I can answer questions about the project, and I like helping my team. At one o'clock, I have lunch and catch up with a colleague. After that, I sort out problems and run my data process. Finally, I wrap up the day and send a short summary to my manager. Every day is different, but I enjoy my work.
